reasoning_model_1: The user's command explicitly requires the model to 'start loudly' and then 'suddenly switch to a low voice/whisper'. Model 1 fails to adhere to the first part of this instruction entirely. From 00:00 to 00:06, the entire utterance is delivered in a hushed, breathy whisper. While this tone suits the second half of the text ('don't tell the boss'), it is completely inappropriate for the first half ('Colleagues please note...'), which was requested to be loud. By whispering the entire script, the model exhibits no dynamic adjustment and ignores the explicit instruction to start loudly.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 follows the structural requirements of the prompt much better than Model 1. From 00:00 to 00:03 ('各位同事...下午三点'), the voice is projected, bright, and maintains an 'announcement' tone, which satisfies the 'start loudly' instruction reasonably well. From 00:04 to 00:06, after the pause, the tone shifts to a more casual and slightly lower volume for the secret part ('不过...'). While the volume drop is not a dramatic whisper—it remains clearly phonated—the directional change in dynamics (from projected to more subdued) is correct and aligns with the semantic shift in the text. It is fluent and natural.
model_comparison:
The core evaluation dimension is 'Dynamic Adjustment' specifically regarding volume. The prompt required a contrast: Loud -> Low. 

Model 1 failed completely on the first half of the instruction. It whispered the entire text, resulting in a flat dynamic profile that missed the requested 'Loud' start. 

Model 2 successfully implemented a dynamic shift. It started with a projected, announcement-style voice (Loud) and switched to a more subdued tone (Low/Softer) for the second half. Although the second half wasn't a strict 'whisper', the contrast was evident and semantically appropriate. Therefore, Model 2 is the clear winner for following the dynamic instruction, whereas Model 1 ignored the dynamic requirement entirely.
